question: Will Reza Pahlavi enter Iran by April 30?
description:
If Reza Pahlavi visits Iran between market creation and April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Reza Pahlavi physically entering the terrestrial territory of Iran. Whether or not Reza Pahlavi enters Iranian airspace or maritime territory during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will Reza Pahlavi enter Iran by...?
events.description:
If Reza Pahlavi visits Iran between market creation and June 30, 2026 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Reza Pahlavi physically entering the terrestrial territory of Iran. Whether or not Reza Pahlavi enters Iranian airspace or maritime territory during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket shows low implied probability for Reza Pahlavi entering Iran by the deadline, primarily due to his continued exile in the US and lack of any announced travel intentions amid Tehran's tight border security. The son of the ousted Shah focuses on overseas advocacy for democratic transition and regime change, recently urging Iran's military to defect amid Israel-Iran escalations and persistent domestic protests. No verified plans for return exist, as physical entry would signal high-risk confrontation with authorities. Key uncertainties include protest momentum and international sanctions pressure; watch for Pahlavi's statements at upcoming forums like the UN General Assembly, though regime stability remains the dominant barrier.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - March 22, 12PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down on March 22?
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT Mar 21 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is lower than the final "Close" price for the Mar 22 '26 12:00 ET candle.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT Mar 21 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is higher than the final "Close" price for the Mar 22 '26 12:00 ET candle.

If the final "Close" price for both of these candles is exactly equal on Binance, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Zadar: Matej Dodig vs Stefano Travaglia
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Matej Dodig and Stefano Travaglia in the Zadar, scheduled for March 21 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Matej Dodig' if Matej Dodig advances against Stefano Travaglia.

This market will resolve to 'Stefano Travaglia' if Stefano Travaglia advances against Matej Dodig.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Stefano Travaglia's higher ATP ranking (around No. 280) and strong clay-court pedigree anchor his 65.5% implied probability against Matej Dodig (unranked ITF-level player) in the Zadar Challenger first round. Travaglia enters with momentum from a recent quarterfinal run in a similar European Challenger, showcasing efficient baseline play and serve holds above 80% on clay. Dodig, a local Croatian wildcard, relies on home crowd support but lacks pro-level experience, with his latest ITF results showing vulnerability to aggressive opponents like Travaglia (2-0 career H2H edge in simulations). No reported injuries for either, though Dodig's lower stamina in longer rallies tilts matchup dynamics toward the Italian veteran, aligning with trader consensus on ranking disparity and surface suitability.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus gives Jesper de Jong a 57% implied probability edge over Roberto Carballes Baena in their Murcia Challenger clay-court matchup, driven primarily by De Jong's recent head-to-head win over the Spaniard in May's Oeiras Challenger (6-3, 7-6). De Jong enters on a hot streak, winning four straight clay matches including a solid 6-3, 6-4 quarterfinal victory over Taberner, showcasing improved baseline consistency and serve hold rates above 85%. Carballes Baena, despite home-crowd support and a strong 6-4, 7-5 win over Rinderknech, has shown vulnerability in tiebreaks against top challengers. No reported injuries for either; odds reflect De Jong's momentum on this surface amid both players' solid recent form.

question: Ethereum Up or Down - March 22, 12PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the ETH/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

question: Solana Up or Down - March 22, 12PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the SOL/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

question: Solana Up or Down on March 22?
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT Mar 21 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is lower than the final "Close" price for the Mar 22 '26 12:00 ET candle.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT Mar 21 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is higher than the final "Close" price for the Mar 22 '26 12:00 ET candle.

If the final "Close" price for both of these candles is exactly equal on Binance, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

description:

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Ghana vs Saint Helena
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Ghana and Saint Helena scheduled for March 27 2026 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Ghana vs Saint Helena - Toss Match Double Ghana Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Ghana and Saint Helena scheduled for 2026-03-27 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Ghana will be considered correct if  Ghana is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Saint Helena will be considered correct if Saint Helena is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: Dogecoin Up or Down - March 22, 12PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the DOGE/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the DOGE/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/DOGE_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance DOGE/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Dogecoin Up or Down on March 22?
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for DOGE/USDT Mar 21 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is lower than the final "Close" price for the Mar 22 '26 12:00 ET candle.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for DOGE/USDT Mar 21 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is higher than the final "Close" price for the Mar 22 '26 12:00 ET candle.

If the final "Close" price for both of these candles is exactly equal on Binance, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the DOGE/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/DOGE_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance DOGE/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Ghana vs Saint Helena - Most Sixes Ghana Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Ghana and Saint Helena scheduled for 2026-03-27 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Ghana will be considered correct if Ghana is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Saint Helena.The outcome corresponding to Saint Helena will be considered correct if Saint Helena is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Ghana.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

The outcome corresponding to Ghana will be considered correct if Ghana is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Saint Helena.The outcome corresponding to Saint Helena will be considered correct if Saint Helena is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Ghana.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

description:
This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Ghana and Saint Helena scheduled for 2026-03-27 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Ghana will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Ghana.
The outcome corresponding to Saint Helena will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Saint Helena.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.

If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Malawi vs Eswatini
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Malawi and Eswatini scheduled for March 27 2026 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Malawi and Eswatini scheduled for 2026-03-27 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Malawi vs Eswatini - Toss Match Double Malawi Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Malawi and Eswatini scheduled for 2026-03-27 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Malawi will be considered correct if  Malawi is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Eswatini will be considered correct if Eswatini is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.

question: HYPE Up or Down - March 22, 12PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the HYPE/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the HYPE/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/futures/HYPEUSDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance HYPE/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: HYPE Up or Down on March 22?
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for HYPE/USDT Mar 21 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is lower than the final "Close" price for the Mar 22 '26 12:00 ET candle.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for HYPE/USDT Mar 21 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is higher than the final "Close" price for the Mar 22 '26 12:00 ET candle.

If the final "Close" price for both of these candles is exactly equal on Binance, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the HYPE/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/futures/HYPEUSDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance HYPE/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tanzania enters as clear trader favorite against Seychelles in the T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B, driven by their dominant group stage form with two convincing wins, including a 104-run thrashing of Gambia, showcasing deep batting led by Abrahim Khaliq and Ivan Selemani. Market pricing reflects Tanzania's 85% implied win probability per crowd wisdom.

events.title: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Seychelles vs Tanzania
events.description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Seychelles and Tanzania scheduled for March 27 2026 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tanzania enters as clear trader favorite against Seychelles in the T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B, driven by their dominant group stage form with two convincing wins, including a 104-run thrashing of Gambia, showcasing deep batting led by Abrahim Khaliq and Ivan Selemani. Seychelles, winless after losses to Ghana and Nigeria, struggle with inconsistent bowling and fragile top order, posting low totals. Head-to-head history favors Tanzania, who won their lone prior meeting by 8 wickets. Entebbe's batsman-friendly pitch and Tanzania's rest advantage post-bye further tilt odds, though Seychelles' desperation could spark an upset in this do-or-die clash for semifinal spots. Market pricing reflects Tanzania's 85% implied win probability per crowd wisdom.
eventMetadata.league: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Seychelles vs Tanzania - Toss Match Double Seychelles Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Seychelles and Tanzania scheduled for 2026-03-27 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Seychelles will be considered correct if  Seychelles is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Tanzania will be considered correct if Tanzania is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: The toss's 50/50 unpredictability anchors trader consensus in this T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B clash, with Seychelles and Tanzania dead even at 49.5% implied probabilities each and a draw/no-result option at 50%, reflecting high variance in short-format outcomes. Both sides boast comparable recent form in Entebbe qualifiers—Tanzania edged recent wins via spin bowling but Seychelles countered with aggressive openers on similar pitches favoring chases. No major injuries or lineup shocks reported, head-to-head limited to one prior Tanzania victory, and neutral weather keep dynamics balanced, as crowds price the captain's call and first-innings adaptation as pivotal swings.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Seychelles vs Tanzania - Most Sixes Seychelles Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Seychelles and Tanzania scheduled for 2026-03-27 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Seychelles will be considered correct if Seychelles is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Tanzania.The outcome corresponding to Tanzania will be considered correct if Tanzania is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Seychelles.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Seychelles, Tanzania, and Draw for most sixes at even 50% odds in this T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B clash, highlighting razor-thin margins in power-hitting potential. Both teams enter with middling recent form—Seychelles notched 5 sixes across two prior qualifiers, Tanzania 6—lacking dominant sluggers amid modest lineups featuring Tanzania's Abhishek Babu (4 sixes in 5 inns) and Seychelles' Krishna Senoy (3 in 4). Flat Entebbe pitches encourage boundaries, but tight bowling units and variable weather keep totals unpredictable, fostering draw risk. No confirmed injuries shift dynamics, leaving historical parity and crowd wisdom to sustain the deadlock.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Seychelles vs Tanzania - Team Top Batter Seychelles Winner
description:
This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Seychelles and Tanzania scheduled for 2026-03-27 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Seychelles will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Seychelles.
The outcome corresponding to Tanzania will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Tanzania.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official batting statistics being recorded, the market will resolve Draw.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects deep uncertainty in this T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B clash between Seychelles and Tanzania, with all outcomes—Seychelles top batter, Tanzania top batter, or draw—evenly priced at 50% due to sparse historical data and volatile short-format dynamics. Both sides feature unproven batting lineups, with Tanzania's Alindile Mokale (recent 40s in qualifiers) facing Seychelles' Grenadine Charles (modest averages under 20), but neither dominates head-to-head or venue stats at Entebbe Oval. Low-scoring pitches and potential rain interruptions heighten draw risk, while Seychelles' home-like advantage tempers Tanzania's superior regional form, keeping the race bunched amid limited beat reporting.

question: T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament, Women: Rwanda vs South Africa
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Rwanda and South Africa scheduled for March 21 2026 in T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament, Women.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament, Women: Rwanda vs South Africa - Toss Match Double Rwanda Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Rwanda and South Africa scheduled for 2026-03-21 in T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament, Women.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Rwanda will be considered correct if  Rwanda is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to South Africa will be considered correct if South Africa is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament, Women: Rwanda vs South Africa - Most Sixes Rwanda Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Rwanda and South Africa scheduled for 2026-03-21 in T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament, Women.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Rwanda will be considered correct if Rwanda is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than South Africa.The outcome corresponding to South Africa will be considered correct if South Africa is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Rwanda.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament, Women: Nigeria vs Ghana
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Nigeria and Ghana scheduled for March 22 2026 in T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament, Women.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nigeria's home advantage and superior recent form in the T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament Women have solidified trader consensus favoring the hosts over Ghana, with implied probabilities reflecting about 70% win odds for Nigeria. The Super Falcons crushed Sierra Leone by 8 wickets in their opener, showcasing explosive batting from opener Salome Sunday and tight bowling led by captain Blessing Etim, while Ghana stumbled to a 5-wicket loss against Zimbabwe, exposing middle-order fragility. Head-to-head, Nigeria holds a 2-1 edge in recent T20Is. Upcoming factors include pitch conditions favoring spin at the Tafawa Balewa Square and Ghana's potential rest for key all-rounder Josephine Obiri, per team updates, though weather remains a wildcard in Abuja.

question: T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament, Women: Nigeria vs Ghana - Toss Match Double Nigeria Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Nigeria and Ghana scheduled for 2026-03-22 in T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament, Women.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Nigeria will be considered correct if  Nigeria is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Ghana will be considered correct if Ghana is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: T20 Series Namibia A vs Uganda: Namibia A vs Uganda
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Namibia A and Uganda scheduled for March 21 2026 in T20 Series Namibia A vs Uganda.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Namibia A's commanding pace attack, led by bowlers claiming 7 wickets in the series opener, has driven trader consensus to favor them at around 65% implied probability in the T20 rubber against Uganda. The hosts defended 168 to win by 20 runs, exploiting Uganda's fragile middle order that crumbled under pressure on a seaming pitch in Windhoek. Uganda, winless in their last three T20Is, struggle chasing totals above 150 historically against Namibia sides. Upcoming decider features a flatter surface favoring batsmen, with Uganda's opener Robinson Obuya in form but spinner Dhruv Deepak sidelined—key factors traders weigh amid rest advantages for the home team.

question: T20 Series Namibia A vs Uganda: Namibia A vs Uganda - Toss Match Double Namibia A Winner
description:

This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Namibia A will be considered correct if  Namibia A is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Uganda will be considered correct if Uganda is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.

If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

description:

question: Pakistan Super League: Lahore Qalandars vs Hyderabad Kingsmen
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Lahore Qalandars and Hyderabad Kingsmen scheduled for March 26 2026 in Pakistan Super League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. eventMetadata.context_description: Lahore Qalandars enter their Pakistan Super League clash with Hyderabad Kingsmen as defending champions but hampered by recent poor form, having finished last in PSL 9 amid bowling injuries to key pacers like Shaheen Afridi. Trader consensus heavily favors Qalandars at home in Lahore, where they boast a strong historical record, implying about 65% win probability amid current odds. Recent squad boosts include retained stars like Fakhar Zaman in top order, while Kingsmen's unproven batting lineup faces scrutiny after draft picks underperformed in practice. Watch for weather in Lahore and pitch curator reports, as spin-friendly conditions could swing momentum; rest advantages minimal with back-to-back scheduling. Upsets remain possible in T20 volatility.

events.title: Pakistan Super League: Lahore Qalandars vs Hyderabad Kingsmen
events.description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Lahore Qalandars and Hyderabad Kingsmen scheduled for March 26 2026 in Pakistan Super League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Recent squad boosts include retained stars like Fakhar Zaman in top order, while Kingsmen's unproven batting lineup faces scrutiny after draft picks underperformed in practice. Watch for weather in Lahore and pitch curator reports, as spin-friendly conditions could swing momentum; rest advantages minimal with back-to-back scheduling. question: Pakistan Super League: Lahore Qalandars vs Hyderabad Kingsmen - Most Sixes Lahore Qalandars Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Lahore Qalandars and Hyderabad Kingsmen scheduled for 2026-03-26 in Pakistan Super League.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Lahore Qalandars will be considered correct if Lahore Qalandars is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Hyderabad Kingsmen.The outcome corresponding to Hyderabad Kingsmen will be considered correct if Hyderabad Kingsmen is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Lahore Qalandars.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: Pakistan Super League: Quetta Gladiators vs Karachi Kings - Toss Match Double Quetta Gladiators Winner
description:
